title: json_skip_number accepts 1. (no fractional digits) — RFC 8259 violation
description: Trigger: a skipped (non-extracted) JSON field whose value is a number with a decimal point but no fractional digits, e.g. `{"count":1.,"password":"hunter2"}`. Bad behavior: extract_field("password") succeeds and returns "hunter2" even though 1. is not a valid JSON number per RFC 8259 §6 (frac = decimal-point 1*DIGIT — one or more digits required after the decimal point). The after-`.` while loop runs zero iterations with no error. Contrast: Python json.loads and jq both reject 1.
